description: Wave 9 - Human Multiplayer v1 (the trimmed 'prove-the-fun' slice from prd-v3.md). Humans play with/against the models in PRIVATE FRIEND LOBBIES, default ANONYMOUS ('guess who is the AI'), always CASUAL (ELO infra designed-now-dormant), WITHOUT compromising the scientific benchmark. Build order: P0 foundations + invariants (121-126) -> P1 identity/consent/durable-state/worker-lane (127-132) -> P2 live transport spine (133-136) -> P3 human-play loop + moderation (137-140) -> P4 disclosure/endgame/stats + CI invariants (141-146) -> P5 lobbies/takeover/cost/sprites/frontend + e2e (147-157). NON-NEGOTIABLE INVARIANTS enforced from US-124/125 onward and gated in CI by US-146: (a) ANONYMITY - no human-vs-AI or model-identity leak on any live/observation/spectator surface before the endgame reveal; guards fail closed to ANONYMOUS. (b) SEGREGATION - a human game writes ZERO rows to the scientific Rating/RatingEvent tables. Human-gated prep (Wave 8 merged to main first; durable-state + worker-lane architecture sign-off; OAuth app credentials; static sprite-library generation; AGENTS.md + prompt.md refresh for this wave; concrete cap/deadline numbers) is NOT stories - see ralph/PREP-v9-human-multiplayer.md. The loop must reach loop-complete without any new credential (tests use mock adapters; OAuth/guard/image keys are deploy-time, not test-time). Several stories (131 durable state, 132 worker lane, 133 live-tail, 140 moderation) are architecturally heavy - if an acceptance criterion proves wrong, fix it in this file and note it in progress.txt rather than silently skipping.

userStories.title: GameSeat human-occupant schema + seat_kind
userStories.description: As the platform, a game seat must be occupiable by a human OR an agent build OR an AI that took over a human seat, so humans and AIs coexist in one game without faking an agent build for a human or polluting agent_build-keyed analytics.
acceptanceCriteria: src/padrino/db/models.py GameSeat: make agent_build_id nullable; add seat_kind str ('AI'|'HUMAN'|'AI_TAKEOVER', NOT NULL, server_default 'AI'); add nullable taken_over_at_phase and takeover_agent_build_id.

ONE Alembic migration after head 0021 begins the Wave 9 chain (all later v9 schema stories chain off it); upgrade/downgrade round-trips on SQLite and Postgres; existing AI-only games are byte-identical (seat_kind='AI', agent_build_id populated).
core/engine/state.py Seat carries an optional seat_kind as pure data with NO change to mechanics; replaying an existing event log reproduces identical state.
Test tests/db/test_game_seat_human_occupant.py asserts schema round-trip, that a legacy AI-only game persists/loads unchanged, and that core replay is unaffected.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: P0 schema lock. Resolves the agent_build_id NOT NULL conflict the review flagged across 4 epics: ONE owner, ONE migration. occupant_principal_id FK is added in US-127 alongside the principals table (kept out of here to avoid a forward dependency).

userStories.title: SeatTakenOver pure core event
userStories.description: As the platform, a silent AI takeover of a human seat must be recorded as a single canonical, hash-chained, replay-stable core event, so provenance is reconstructable and the imitation game is never broken mid-play.
acceptanceCriteria: core/engine/events.py: add SeatTakenOver (visibility=SYSTEM; payload public_player_id, day, phase, reason, replacement_agent_build_ref) to the Event discriminated union, EVENT_TYPES, EventAdapter, and __all__; NO wall-clock or random in the payload.

core/engine/reducer.py apply_event folds SeatTakenOver as provenance-only (state-preserving, no mechanics mutation); a pure helper compute_seat_provenance(event_log) -> {public_player_id: 'HUMAN'|'AI'|'HUMAN_THEN_AI'}.
Folding a log containing SeatTakenOver reproduces identical state and a valid hash chain; the purity-firewall test covers the new code (no random/datetime/IO under core/).
Test tests/core/test_seat_takeover_event.py covers the fold, provenance derivation, and replay/hash stability.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: P0. One canonical takeover event - the review found 3 epics inventing it under different names.

userStories.title: Out-of-band human-chat sidecar (off the hash chain)
userStories.description: As the platform, human free-text chat (PII) must live outside the hash-chained event log so GDPR erasure can redact it WITHOUT breaking deterministic replay - which is mathematically impossible if the raw text is inside a hashed event.
acceptanceCriteria: New table human_chat_messages (db/models.py): id, game_id FK, sequence (pairs with the GameEvent sequence), public_player_id, raw_text, cleaned_text, redacted bool default false, created_at. Raw human chat text lives ONLY here.

For human seats, the paired PublicMessageSubmitted/PrivateMessageSubmitted core event carries only a content reference/hash, not the raw text, so redacting the sidecar never changes any event_hash; the chat-firewall (chat drives no mechanics) is preserved.
A redact(game_id, sequence) operation nulls raw/cleaned text and sets redacted=true without touching game_events; verify_chain still passes afterward.
Migration chains after US-121; test tests/db/test_human_chat_sidecar.py asserts the hash chain is unchanged before/after redaction and that raw human text never appears in game_events.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: P0. Fixes the GDPR-vs-hash-chain impossibility all three reviewers independently flagged.

userStories.title: Anonymity guard: human markers + column-level projection guard
userStories.description: As the platform, no human-vs-AI or model-identity signal may leak on any observation/public surface before the endgame reveal, and the guard must catch new identity DB COLUMNS, not only payload keys.
acceptanceCriteria: core/observation_privacy.py: extend FORBIDDEN_PAYLOAD_KEYS (and the spectator_projection alias) with the human-identity set: is_human, controller_type, seat_kind, occupant_principal_id, occupant_user_id, human_player_id, takeover, taken_over_at_phase, takeover_agent_build_id.

Add an allowlist-based DB-column projection guard so any seat/game row reaching a public or observation surface is projected through a field allowlist - a new identity COLUMN cannot leak even though it is not a payload key; assert_anonymous_safe(payload) raises on any forbidden key.
Guards FAIL CLOSED: a missing/None identity_mode coerces to ANONYMOUS (strip), never TRANSPARENT.
Property-test scaffold tests/core/test_anonymity_property.py (hypothesis) scans representative observation + public frames for forbidden keys and runs in the default suite.

A hard segregation test tests/ratings/test_segregation.py proves completing a human-lane game writes ZERO rows to scientific Rating/RatingEvent and ZERO to human_rating (casual/dormant in v1).

db Game gains identity_mode (NOT NULL, server_default 'ANONYMOUS'), frozen after game start; legacy AI games default ANONYMOUS with no behavior change.

A single pure function composition_summary(seats) -> {human_count, ai_count, total} (data-in, no IO) is the ONLY producer of composition counts consumed by lobby, live, spectator, and observation surfaces.

userStories.title: Human principals, sessions, and get_human_context
userStories.description: As the platform, browser humans need an identity/session layer that is completely separate from the existing API-key auth, and a game seat must be linkable to a human principal.
acceptanceCriteria: New tables principals (id, kind 'guest'|'account', display_name nullable, deleted_at nullable, created_at/updated_at) and human_sessions (id, principal_id FK, session_hash sha256 only, kind, issued_at, expires_at, revoked_at, last_seen_at); add nullable occupant_principal_id (FK principals.id) to game_seats.

New src/padrino/api/human_auth.py: HumanPrincipalContext + get_human_context resolving the session/guest cookie WITHOUT consulting api_keys; require_human()/require_account() (401 vs 403). api/auth.py get_auth_context is unchanged and provably non-overlapping (a guest cookie grants zero API scope and vice-versa).
Per-human-principal rate limiting reuses RateLimitStore with a new ceiling padrino_rate_limit_human_per_minute.
Migration chains after US-126; tests/api/test_human_auth.py covers guest/account/expired/revoked, guest-on-account-only-route (403), and non-overlap with API keys in both directions.

userStories.title: Guest quickplay (no signup)
userStories.description: As an invited friend with no account, I want to join from an invite link and immediately get a usable identity, so I can play without any signup.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /human/guest (new router src/padrino/api/routes/human.py) creates a guest principal, issues an http-only+Secure+SameSite=Lax cookie holding an opaque token (persisted only as sha256, constant-time compared), and returns a guest summary; reachable even when create_app(auth_required=True).
PATCH /human/me sets a per-session display_name (validated, not globally unique); GET /human/me returns the current principal.
Creating a guest never touches api_keys and grants no scope; the plaintext token never appears in any DB column.
tests/api/test_guest_quickplay.py covers creation, cookie attributes, display-name set, and absence of plaintext token.

userStories.title: Optional OAuth sign-in (one provider)
userStories.description: As a returning player, I want to sign in with one provider so my stats persist across sessions/devices - without any friends graph or multi-account merge in v1.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /human/oauth/{provider}/start and /callback (Authlib server-side code flow, ONE provider e.g. Google) with CSRF state + PKCE; find-or-create an account principal keyed by (provider, subject); issue an account session.
Provider client id/secret read from settings (optional/None so the engine boots and tests run without them) and never logged; no provider tokens persisted beyond completing the exchange.
A signed-in guest is upgraded in place: the single active guest principal's sessions and in-flight lobby membership re-point to the account; NO multi-guest merge, NO friends graph.
authlib added to deps with justification in progress.txt; tests/api/test_oauth_signin.py uses a stubbed provider (no network).

The first human action OR chat submission is rejected (HTTP 412/409) unless a current consent + 16+ acknowledgement exists for that principal; enforced in api/runner, never in core; a document_version bump re-prompts.
tests/api/test_consent_gate.py: pre-consent action rejected, post-consent allowed, version bump re-prompts.

userStories.title: Durable, rehydratable human-game state
userStories.description: As the operator, a process restart must not lose an in-progress human game - which can last minutes to hours - so human-lane game state must be durable and rehydratable from the database.
acceptanceCriteria: New table human_game_runtime (game_id, phase, deadline_at, buffer_snapshot JSON, updated_at) holding ONLY impure live scaffolding; deterministic core state is always reconstructed from the hash-chained event log, never from the snapshot.

rehydrate_active_human_games() rebuilds runner state for every in-progress human game from the event log + the runtime row and resumes the current phase; uses the existing async DB (NO new Redis dependency - stack rule).
tests/runner/test_human_game_durability.py: simulate a mid-game restart and assert core-state replay equality and phase resumption with no lost events.
Migration chains after US-130.

The CLI gains an entrypoint (padrino human-lane, or a flag) to run the human lane as its own process.
tests/runner/test_human_lane_isolation.py asserts human games consume human-lane slots only and that many waiting human lobbies do not reduce benchmark concurrency.

userStories.title: Live-tail SSE for in-progress games
userStories.description: As the platform, the SSE spine must stream an IN-PROGRESS game (today it only paces a finished game's committed log then ends), so humans and spectators see the game as it happens.
acceptanceCriteria: A live-tail mode streams the committed prefix then continues emitting newly committed PUBLIC events as produced, with keep-alive heartbeats and Last-Event-ID/?after= resume on a still-growing log; mid-game mark_live works (today mark_live only runs post-completion); uses short read transactions / poll-append, no long-held DB session.
Reuses the existing public_event_v1 projection + FORBIDDEN_PAYLOAD_KEYS (identity-blind); a LIVE game never exposes terminal_result.
tests/public/test_live_tail.py: a growing log is tailed, resume-by-sequence yields no gaps/dupes, the terminal frame closes the stream.

userStories.title: Authenticated POST action channel
userStories.description: As a human player, I submit structured actions (vote/protect/investigate/etc.) over an authenticated POST channel, validated server-side, with safe retries.
acceptanceCriteria: An authenticated POST (human session) submits a structured Action {type, target?} (exactly core/engine/actions.py) for the caller's seat; validated against legal_actions_for and the chat firewall (only Action mutates state); illegal/out-of-phase/wrong-seat rejected.
An idempotency key dedupes retries (lightweight store keyed by game+seat+phase+key) so a network retry never double-votes.
tests/api/test_human_action_channel.py covers accept-once-idempotent, illegal-rejected, out-of-phase-rejected, wrong-seat-rejected.

userStories.description: As a human player, I submit chat over an authenticated POST channel; the message enters the buffered hold and is released only after moderation passes.
acceptanceCriteria: An authenticated POST submits a public/private chat message respecting message_limits; the message enters the buffer hold and is gated by the block-before-release moderation hook (US-140) before any release; idempotency key dedupes retries.
Raw human chat text is routed to the sidecar (US-123), never inline in a hash-chained payload.
tests/api/test_human_chat_channel.py covers over-limit-rejected, held-then-released, idempotent.

Reuses build_observation from the impure layer (no new branching in core); in anonymous mode the stream contains no forbidden keys.
tests/api/test_seat_observation_stream.py: a seat sees only its own private info + legal actions, the deadline frame is present, and no forbidden keys appear in anonymous mode.

userStories.title: HumanAdapter satisfies the LlmAdapter protocol
userStories.description: As the runner, a human seat must be drivable through the exact same adapter contract as an LLM, so the tick barrier is unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: New src/padrino/llm/human_adapter.py implements the LlmAdapter Protocol: complete(observation) resolves the seat's turn from buffered human input (the POST channels) within the per-phase deadline, or coerces to a safe action (NOOP/ABSTAIN) on timeout - exactly like a misbehaving LLM; no tick.py contract change.
Illegal human submissions are coerced to a safe action against legal_actions_for, same as a malformed LLM response.
tests/llm/test_human_adapter.py covers buffered-resolve, timeout-coerce, illegal-coerce.

userStories.title: Human-aware tick + symmetric fixed release delay
userStories.description: As the platform, the tick must wait on slow humans under human-friendly deadlines and release ALL public messages (human and AI) on the same fixed delay, so message timing cannot out a seat.
acceptanceCriteria: A human-aware tick awaits HumanAdapter and LLM seats under per-phase deadlines, then applies a FIXED release delay symmetrically to ALL public messages - humans AND AI - so no per-side timing signal exists (decision 5, simplified: fixed delay, NO seeded jitter / multi-message windows in v1). Settings: padrino_human_phase_deadline_seconds, padrino_human_release_delay_seconds.
All delay/clock logic lives in the impure runner (injected clock); core stays pure and the purity-firewall test passes.
tests/runner/test_human_tick_symmetry.py asserts AI and human public messages release on the same schedule and the phase always settles within deadline+delay.

userStories.title: Mixed human+AI seats + replay determinism
userStories.description: As the platform, one game may mix human and AI seats and must still replay bit-for-bit from its event log.
acceptanceCriteria: SeatMultiplexAdapter wires a single game with a per-seat mix of HumanAdapter and LLM adapters; an AI takeover swaps a seat's adapter between ticks.
All human non-determinism (submissions, release ordering, takeover) is captured as committed events; clocks/RNG stay in the runner.
tests/runner/test_mixed_seat_replay.py asserts a completed mixed human+AI game replays to an identical hash-chained state.

userStories.title: Block-before-release moderation (hardened fail path)
userStories.description: As the platform, every human message must pass moderation inside the buffer hold window before any other player sees it, and a moderation outage must never halt the game.
acceptanceCriteria: A real-time gate runs inside the hold window: deterministic first-pass (extract public/moderation.deterministic_first_pass as a pure single-message verdict) + async guard model, under a hard latency budget; the pure sanitizer (core/agents/sanitizer.py) is wired to live human input; verdict taxonomy ALLOW/SOFT_MASK/BLOCK with a pure deterministic span-mask.

HARDENED fail path: the deterministic first-pass is the instant backstop; on guard timeout/error, fall back to the deterministic-only verdict for THAT message (or hold only that message) - the game NEVER halts; a BLOCK is never released and never chained; release timing follows the symmetric delay independent of the moderation path (no timing leak).
Per-user and per-game/phase message rate limits via RateLimitStore; released/masked text persists to the sidecar (US-123); raw text never reaches another seat or spectator.

tests/public/test_live_moderation.py: blocked message never released; guard-timeout falls back and the game proceeds; a recording stub proves the gate actually saw the message (guards the Wave-7 vacuous-gate class of bug).
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: P3. Decision 12 preserved; the fail path the reviewers flagged is hardened. Latency hides inside the existing buffer window.

userStories.title: Identity-mode-aware observation + projection

A missing/None mode coerces to ANONYMOUS (fail closed).
tests/core/test_mode_aware_observation.py: anonymous leaks nothing, transparent surfaces allowed fields, None coerces to anonymous.

userStories.title: Endgame reveal projection + terminal-gated endpoint

GET /public/games/{id}/reveal returns it ONLY when the game is terminal/RECENT; 404/empty while LIVE; always reveals even in anonymous mode (decision 11).

userStories.title: Spot-the-AI guess + pure scorer + personal stat
userStories.description: As a human player, after the game I guess which seats were AI and see my detection accuracy - the core imitation-game hook - without any competitive leaderboard in v1.

The reveal endpoint gates the viewer's own accuracy result behind their submitted guess; NO cross-player/cross-model leaderboards in v1.

userStories.notes: P4. Stats captured (accounts persist them); leaderboards/ELO deferred to a fast-follow.

userStories.title: Anonymity + segregation CI gates
userStories.description: As the platform, the two non-negotiable invariants must be suite-blocking so no future change can regress anonymity or pollute the benchmark.
acceptanceCriteria: Promote the anonymity property test (US-124) and segregation test (US-125) to comprehensive gates covering every live frame, observation, pre-terminal reveal, and export path: a completed human game provably leaks zero human-vs-AI/model markers pre-reveal and writes zero scientific rating rows.
tests/test_invariants_human_mp.py aggregates the property + segregation checks and runs in the default suite.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: P4. The two invariants enforced in CI from here on (the review's #3 top risk).

userStories.title: Lobby tables + create/configure
userStories.description: As a host, I create a private lobby and configure its game (ruleset/size, identity mode, theme, bot pre-pick vs auto-fill, casual stakes).
acceptanceCriteria: New tables lobbies, lobby_members, lobby_seats (per the schema in prd-v3.md): lobby holds ruleset_id, identity_mode, theme_pack_id, stakes pinned CASUAL, status OPEN|LOCKED|LAUNCHED|CLOSED, lobby_seed, host, league_id (Humans-Included), nullable game_id.

POST /lobbies (create + host config: ruleset/size in {mini7_v1, bench10_v1}, identity_mode, theme_pack_id from the static library, pre-pick human_eligible models OR curated auto-fill, stakes=CASUAL); GET /lobbies/{id} member-scoped with count-only composition.
Migration chains after the latest v9 schema head; tests/api/test_lobby_create.py.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: P5. Private friend lobbies only (decision 2). No public matchmaking.

userStories.title: Invite links, roster, ready-up, presence
userStories.description: As a host, I share an invite link; friends (guest or signed-in) join, ready up, and I can lock and launch; idle lobbies auto-cancel.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /lobbies/join/{invite_token} (guest- and account-joinable, single-use-per-person via membership), roster read, ready-up, host lock/kick/transfer; heartbeat presence with stale-member eviction; idle/host-abandon auto-cancel lifecycle.
A member-scoped lobby state channel streams roster/ready/presence deltas, identity-blind (never per-seat human/AI in anonymous mode).
tests/api/test_lobby_invite_presence.py covers join, ready, presence eviction, and auto-cancel.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Deterministic curated auto-fill + launch handoff
userStories.description: As the platform, empty seats fill deterministically from a curated cheap-model pool and the lobby launches a real game on the human worker lane.
acceptanceCriteria: Pure src/padrino/core/lobby/autofill.py deterministically assigns curated human_eligible AgentBuild ids to EMPTY seats given (lobby_seed, reserved map, curated roster), SeededRng only (no random/clock/IO), raising when the pool is smaller than the empty seats.

Launch handoff materializes a Game + GameSeat rows (humans -> occupant_principal_id with seat_kind HUMAN, AI -> agent_build_id with seat_kind AI) on the human worker lane (US-132); launch is single-fire/idempotent.
tests/core/test_lobby_autofill.py (purity + determinism) and tests/api/test_lobby_launch.py (idempotent handoff).
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Disconnect grace + silent AI takeover
userStories.description: As a player, if I drop I get a short grace window to reconnect; otherwise an AI silently takes my seat so the game continues, invisibly in anonymous mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-seat presence over the human transport; a reconnect grace window with identity-blind full-state replay; on grace expiry a curated AI silently takes over (SeatMultiplexAdapter swap; emit SeatTakenOver; reveal-only). Reconnect before takeover returns the seat; after takeover returns an identity-blind spectator.
In anonymous mode, other-seat presence/reconnecting state is never exposed (AIs do not disconnect); takeover is invisible mid-game and appears only in the endgame reveal provenance.
tests/runner/test_disconnect_takeover.py: the game progresses through a takeover, no mid-game leak, provenance shown at reveal.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: P5. Decision 9 (v1 casual; ranked leaver penalties dormant).

userStories.title: Cost governance: caps + throttle-not-kill breaker
userStories.description: As the operator, human play is platform-absorbed within a Moderate budget; caps and a circuit breaker bound spend by stopping NEW lobbies, never by killing an active game.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-user/day game + join caps and a per-user/day inference-$ cap keyed on principal (OAuth account else hashed guest token), enforced at lobby create/join/launch admission; curated human_eligible model pool; fallback token-price table when LiteLLM response_cost is None.

A per-lobby cost cap + circuit breaker that, on breach, STOPS new lobbies / new LLM turns but LETS ACTIVE GAMES FINISH (never boots humans); add a funding_source column (PLATFORM|BYOK_OWNER|SPONSOR_POOL, default PLATFORM) to the cost-tracking row (BYOK/sponsor otherwise dormant).
tests/economics/test_human_cost_governance.py: admission denies past caps, the breaker throttles new lobbies but never kills an active game, funding_source is recorded.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: P5. Decisions 14/19/20. The reviewers' 'AI-only continuation boots humans' anti-pattern is explicitly rejected. Concrete cap numbers are a PREP-v9 sign-off.

userStories.title: Static themed sprite library
userStories.description: As a player, seats show themed sprites from a curated static library - no runtime image generation - and in anonymous mode the sprite can never encode role or human/AI.
acceptanceCriteria: A curated set of pre-generated themed sprite assets (3-5 theme packs) shipped as static files + a manifest, served read-only with immutable Cache-Control via a new asset route; anonymous mode maps every seat to a role-agnostic archetype per theme; deterministic placeholder fallback.
NO runtime image generation in v1 (the gemini-3.1-flash-image authoring step is offline/human - see PREP-v9).
tests/api/test_sprite_library.py: the manifest serves, anonymous archetypes are role-agnostic, an unknown key 404s.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: P5. Decision 13 delivered as a static library; the review cut the runtime pipeline.

userStories.title: Frontend live transport client
userStories.description: As the dashboard, I gain a bidirectional play client (SSE-out + authenticated POST-in) with reconnect-by-sequence and a human session credential, turning the observe-only app interactive.
acceptanceCriteria: web/dashboard: a liveClient (SSE-out + authenticated POST-in) with reconnect-by-sequence + idempotency keys, and a play-session store (Svelte 5 runes) deriving board/votes/chat from the released frame stream; PadrinoClient gains a human session/guest credential mode alongside the existing spectator API key.

web/dashboard/src/lib/api gains types + client methods for the live seat stream, action POST, chat POST, lobby, reveal, guess, and human stats; vitest unit coverage parallels the existing client tests.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes

uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Frontend in-game play surface
userStories.description: As a player, I see the board, my legal actions, the phase timer, and a buffered chat that shows messages only after release - all identity-blind in anonymous mode.
acceptanceCriteria: /play/[gameId]: seat board (identity-blind, count-only composition, themed static sprites), phase + countdown header (non-precise so it does not leak timing), legal-action-gated action panel + vote panel + role-conditional night panel, a buffered chat composer with pending/blocked/released states and a feed fed ONLY by released frames; reconnect with full-state replay.
Playwright web/dashboard/tests/e2e/play.spec.ts covers a scripted game turn (vote + a released chat message) and asserts no identity leak in anonymous mode.

userStories.title: Frontend endgame reveal + guess + profile
userStories.description: As a player, at game end I see the full reveal, submit my spot-the-AI guess, see my detection accuracy, and (if signed in) view my stats.
acceptanceCriteria: Endgame reveal screen (per-seat human/AI, role, faction, exact model, takeover provenance, themed sprite); the spot-the-AI guess UI (per-seat HUMAN/AI toggles, single submit, gating the reveal of own accuracy); a minimal profile/stats page for signed-in accounts (casual framing, no live ELO).
Playwright web/dashboard/tests/e2e/reveal.spec.ts covers guess-then-reveal and the stats page.

userStories.title: End-to-end human-game smoke test
userStories.description: As the platform, one default-suite test must exercise the ENTIRE human spine - lobby through reveal - so a cross-boundary contract break can never pass a green suite (the Wave-7 lesson).
acceptanceCriteria: tests/integration/test_human_game_e2e.py (default suite; mock LLM adapter + a scripted human client driving the POST channels; zero/short delays): create lobby -> guest join -> consent -> ready -> auto-fill -> play a full game through votes + night actions + one moderated chat message -> terminal -> endgame reveal exposes models + human seats -> a spot-the-AI guess scores.
The test asserts anonymity held on every live frame (no forbidden keys) and that ZERO scientific Rating/RatingEvent rows were written.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: P5 verification. The single end-to-end guard for the whole human lane; closes the wave.
